probes.name: create_goal_with_priority_and_level
probes.prompt: Please add a goal called 'Prepare for System Design Interview' with high priority. Break it down into sub-goals: one to practice designing distributed caching systems, one to review load balancing strategies, and one to do a mock interview by end of next week.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as a goal interface. A good response creates the parent goal 'Prepare for System Design Interview' with high priority, creates the three specified sub-goals linked to it, sets appropriate deadlines, confirms each creation, avoids duplicates, and emits a workflow_result confirmation summarizing all created goals.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #9 (breaking down salary negotiation into sub-goals) and #38 (adding two tasks with specific details like calling mother and researching pay).

probes.name: list_goals_with_hierarchy_request
probes.prompt: Can you fetch all my current goals and show me the full hierarchy? I want to see which goals have sub-goals under them, and I'd like to know if there are any goals related to 'Prostownik' or 'KsEF' still active.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as a goal interface. A good response fetches the complete goal hierarchy, displays parent-child relationships clearly, identifies any goals matching 'Prostownik' or 'KsEF' in the results, and emits a workflow_result confirmation with the structured hierarchy data.

probes.name: mark_done_with_pattern_matching
probes.prompt: Please mark all tasks related to setting up landing pages and any goals connected to the 'Prostownik' project as done. I've finished building the mobile app prototype for the depilator and the landing pages are live now.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as a goal interface. A good response searches for goals matching 'landing pages' and 'Prostownik', marks each matched goal's status as done, confirms which specific goals were updated, handles the case where no matches exist gracefully, avoids marking unrelated goals, and emits a workflow_result confirmation listing all status changes.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

probes.name: remove_duplicate_and_test_goals
probes.prompt: Hej, I noticed I have some leftover test goals and duplicates in the system. Please remove any goals that are clearly test entries or duplicates. Specifically, there should be two test goals named 'Test goal 1' and 'Test goal' — remove those. Also if there's a duplicate of 'Download Qwen model' please remove the duplicate one only.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as a goal interface. A good response searches for the specified test goals and the duplicate 'Download Qwen model' goal, removes the test goals entirely, identifies which of the duplicate entries to remove (keeping the original), confirms each deletion with goal names/IDs, and emits a workflow_result confirmation summarizing all removals.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #14 (remove test goals), #23 (remove not real tasks), #25 (duplicate task), and #34 (download model reference).

probes.name: update_goal_status_and_reschedule
probes.prompt: Mam kilka zmian. First, my master thesis defense goal — please update it to in-progress status and set the deadline to next Friday at 18:00. Second, the 'go for a walk' recurring goal should be set to active again and rescheduled to 17:00 daily. Third, can you check if there's already a goal about 'registering free time at work' and if not, create one with medium priority due by end of this month?
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as a goal interface. A good response: (1) finds the thesis defense goal, updates status to in-progress and sets deadline to next Friday 18:00, (2) finds the walking goal, sets it to active and reschedules to 17:00 daily, (3) checks for an existing 'registering free time at work' goal and either updates it or creates a new one with medium priority and end-of-month deadline, avoids creating duplicates, and emits a workflow_result confirmation covering all three operations.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #31 (rescheduling walk and thesis defense with times), #36 (take time off work/register free time), #5 (thesis task), and #38 (multiple task operations in one message).
